entries.description: Create symbolic link. -s makes it symbolic (soft link) not hard. Target is the original file, link is the new path. Use -f to overwrite existing link. Use readlink -f link to see where a symlink points.

Generate SSH key pair. -t ed25519 is more secure and faster than rsa. -C adds a comment/label. Saved to ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 (private) and .pub (public). Use -f to specify output path. Add to agent: ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519
Check disk usage of each item in current directory. -s summarizes (hides subdirs), -h human-readable (KB/MB/GB). Find largest: du -sh * | sort -rh | head -20
Check total size of a specific folder. Add --max-depth=1 to break down one level of subdirectories. du -ah shows all files including hidden ones.

Find files by name pattern. -type f limits to files (d=dirs, l=symlinks). -iname for case-insensitive. -mtime -7 for modified in last 7 days. -size +100M for files over 100MB. -exec cmd {} \; to run a command on each result.
Check system RAM and swap usage. -h human-readable. Shows total/used/free/shared/buff-cache/available. "available" is the practical free memory (more useful than "free").

Show GPU utilization, memory, temperature, and running processes. Monitor live: watch -n 1 nvidia-smi. Parseable: nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name,memory.used,memory.total,utilization.gpu --format=csv,noheader
Check filesystem disk space. -h human-readable. Shows size/used/available/use%/mountpoint per filesystem. Add -T to show filesystem type (ext4, nfs, etc).
Make file executable. +x adds execute for user/group/other. u+x for user only. Numeric: 755 (rwxr-xr-x, public executable), 644 (rw-r--r--), 700 (private executable). -R to recurse into dirs.
Compress folder to tar.gz. -c create, -z gzip, -f filename. -j for bzip2 (.tar.bz2), -J for xz (.tar.xz). --exclude="*.log" to skip patterns. List contents without extracting: tar -tzf archive.tar.gz
Extract tar.gz archive. -x extract, -z gunzip, -f filename. -C /target/dir to extract to a specific directory. -v to list files as extracted. Strip top-level dir: --strip-components=1

Sync files. -a archive (preserves permissions/timestamps/symlinks), -v verbose, -z compress during transfer. --dry-run to preview. --delete removes files not in source. Trailing / on source = contents only (not the dir itself).
Find process using a specific port. Useful for "port already in use" errors. Kill it: kill $(lsof -t -i :8080). Alternatives: netstat -tlnp | grep 8080  or  ss -tlnp | grep 8080
List all branches. -a shows local and remote tracking branches. -v adds last commit info. Delete: git branch -d name (safe) or -D (force). Show merged: git branch --merged

Create and switch to new branch in one step. Equivalent to: git branch new-branch && git checkout new-branch. From a specific commit: git checkout -b new-branch abc1234. Modern alias: git switch -c new-branch

Visual commit history with ASCII branch graph. --oneline: one line per commit. --graph: draw tree. --all: show all branches. Limit: -n 20. Filter by author: --author="name". Filter by date: --after="2024-01-01". Show files changed: --stat

Temporarily save uncommitted changes then restore. Other commands: stash list (see all), stash drop (discard top), stash apply (restore without removing), stash push -m "msg" (label it), stash show -p (see diff)

Show changes staged but not yet committed. Without --staged: shows unstaged changes. git diff HEAD: all changes vs last commit. git diff branch1..branch2: compare branches. Add -- file.txt to limit to a specific file.
List remote repositories with fetch/push URLs. remote add name url: add remote. remote set-url origin new-url: change URL. remote prune origin: clean up stale remote-tracking branches.
Apply a specific commit from another branch to current branch. -n: apply without committing. --abort to cancel if conflicts. Range: git cherry-pick A..B applies all commits after A up to B.

Interactive rebase last 3 commits. Squash, reorder, edit, or drop commits. Commands: pick (keep), squash/s (combine), fixup/f (squash, discard message), drop/d (remove), edit/e (pause to amend). Do not rebase already-pushed commits without force-push.

Undo last commit but keep changes staged. --mixed (default): unstage too. --hard: discard everything (destructive). HEAD~2 to go back 2 commits. Recover from mistakes: git reflog then git reset --hard <hash>
Create new named session. -s sets name. -d creates detached (background). -n names first window. Check if exists in scripts: tmux has-session -t name 2>/dev/null
Attach to existing session by name or index. -d detaches other clients first (single-display mode). Attach to most recent: tmux a. List sessions first: tmux ls
Kill a specific session and all its windows/panes. kill-server kills everything. kill-window -t session:1 kills just window 1. Prefix key + & kills current window. Prefix key + x kills current pane.
List all running sessions with window count and time. Shorthand: tmux ls. Shows attached/detached status. Check existence in scripts: tmux has-session -t name 2>/dev/null && echo exists

Split current pane horizontally (left/right panes). -v splits vertically (top/bottom). -p 30 sets new pane to 30% of space. -c /path sets working dir. Prefix + % (horizontal) or " (vertical) also works.
Split current pane vertically (top/bottom panes). Navigate panes: prefix + arrow keys. Resize: prefix + Ctrl+arrow. Zoom pane fullscreen toggle: prefix + z. Swap panes: prefix + { or }.
Create new window in current session. -n names it. -c /path sets working dir. Switch windows: prefix + [0-9] or prefix + n/p (next/prev). Rename current window: prefix + ,
Arrange all panes in tiled layout. Options: even-horizontal, even-vertical, main-horizontal, main-vertical, tiled. Prefix + Space cycles through layouts. Useful after splitting many panes.

entries.command: ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=width,height -of csv=p=0 input.mp4
entries.description: Get video dimensions (W,H). -v error suppresses banner. -select_streams v:0 targets first video stream. -of csv=p=0 outputs plain CSV without headers. Output: 1920,1080

entries.command: ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=r_frame_rate -of csv=p=0 input.mp4
entries.description: Get video frame rate. Returns as fraction: 30/1 = 30fps, 30000/1001 ~= 29.97fps. For file duration: ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of csv=p=0 input.mp4
Show all media metadata as JSON. show_format: duration, bitrate, format, file size. show_streams: codec, resolution, fps, sample_rate per stream. Pipe to jq: ffprobe ... | jq .streams[0].codec_name

Extract audio without re-encoding. -vn disables video. -acodec copy streams audio as-is (fast, no quality loss). Output format set by extension (.aac, .mp3, .wav, .opus). Use -ab 192k to transcode to a specific bitrate instead.
Remove audio track. -an disables audio. -vcodec copy keeps video without re-encoding (fast). Result is a silent video. To replace audio, use the combine command.

entries.command: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -i audio.aac -c:v copy -c:a aac -map 0:v:0 -map 1:a:0 output.mp4
entries.description: Combine video with new audio. -map 0:v:0 takes video from first input, -map 1:a:0 takes audio from second. -c:v copy avoids re-encoding video (fast). Add -shortest to stop at the shorter stream.

Crop video to region. Format: crop=out_w:out_h:x:y (x,y = top-left corner). Use expressions: crop=iw/2:ih/2:0:0 (top-left quarter). Auto-detect black bars: first run with -vf cropdetect, then use reported values.
Crop center 50% of video. iw/ih are input width/height as expressions. iw/4:ih/4 centers the crop (offsets 25% from each edge). For 75%: crop=iw*3/4:ih*3/4:iw/8:ih/8

Resize video. Use -1 for auto dimension (preserves aspect ratio): scale=1280:-1. Use -2 to ensure even dimensions (required by some codecs): scale=1280:-2. Fit in bounds with letterbox: scale=1280:720:force_original_aspect_ratio=decrease,pad=1280:720:(ow-iw)/2:(oh-ih)/2

Trim video by timestamp. -ss start, -to end time. Use -t for duration instead: -t 00:01:00. -c copy skips re-encoding (fast, but cut may be slightly imprecise at keyframe boundaries). Remove -c copy for frame-accurate cuts (slower, re-encodes).

Trim video by frame number (0-indexed). select/aselect filter frames, setpts/asetpts reset timestamps so output starts at 0. For video-only, replace -af part with -an. Get frame numbers first with ffprobe.

Extract video frames as images. fps=1 = 1 frame/sec. fps=0.5 = one every 2 sec. fps=25 = all frames. %04d produces frame_0001.png, frame_0002.png. Extract just the first frame: -vf "select=eq(n\,0)" -vframes 1 first.png

Convert video to high-quality GIF with optimized palette. fps=10: frame rate (lower = smaller file). scale=320:-1: resize width to 320. palettegen+paletteuse produces much better colors than simple conversion. Increase scale for larger GIF.

entries.command: ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -c:v libx264 -crf 23 -preset medium -c:a aac -b:a 128k output.mp4
entries.description: Re-encode video with H.264. -crf: quality (0=lossless, 18=visually lossless, 23=default, 51=worst). -preset: ultrafast/fast/medium/slow/veryslow (slower = smaller file, same quality). -b:a: audio bitrate. Use libx265 for H.265/HEVC (better compression).
Get image dimensions (WxH). Format codes: %w=width, %h=height, %z=bit depth, %m=format, %b=file size. Use identify -verbose image.png for all metadata. Works on JPG, PNG, TIFF, WebP, etc.

Crop image to region. Format: WxH+X+Y where X,Y is top-left offset. 100x100 pixel area starting at (50,50). Add +repage after crop to reset virtual canvas. -gravity Center -crop 100x100+0+0 crops from center.

Crop center 50% of image. -gravity Center positions crop in the middle. 50%x50% takes half width and height. +repage removes virtual canvas offset artifacts. Use -gravity NorthWest for top-left origin.

Resize image preserving aspect ratio (fits within 800x600). 800x600! forces exact size (may distort). 800x600> only shrinks if larger. 800x600^ fills bounds (may overflow, pair with -gravity and -extent). 50% to scale by percentage.
Create virtual environment in .venv dir. Specify Python version: uv venv --python 3.11. Much faster than python -m venv (written in Rust). Creates .venv/bin/activate and .venv/bin/python.
Activate virtual environment. fish shell: source .venv/bin/activate.fish. Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate. Deactivate: deactivate. Check active env: which python  or  echo $VIRTUAL_ENV

Install Python package (much faster than pip, uses parallel downloads). Multiple: uv pip install pkg1 pkg2. From file: uv pip install -r requirements.txt. Editable: uv pip install -e . Skip cache: --no-cache
List installed packages with pinned versions (pip freeze format). Generate requirements: uv pip freeze > requirements.txt. Useful for auditing venv contents.

Resolve and pin all dependencies. Takes loose requirements.in and produces fully pinned requirements.txt. --upgrade to update all. --upgrade-package pkg for one package. --generate-hashes for secure reproducible installs.

Run Python script with automatic dependency handling. Reads inline script metadata (PEP 723) or pyproject.toml. No manual venv activation needed. Specify Python: uv run --python 3.11 script.py. Run tools: uv run pytest

Create new conda environment with specific Python. -n names it. Include packages upfront: conda create -n myenv python=3.11 numpy pandas. -y skips confirmation. --clone existing_env to copy an environment.
Activate conda environment. Requires conda init (one-time setup). conda activate with no name goes to base. Check current: echo $CONDA_DEFAULT_ENV  or  conda info
Deactivate current environment, return to base or system Python. Can be called multiple times if activations were nested.
List all conda environments with their paths. Active env marked with *. Shorthand: conda info --envs. Check sizes: du -sh ~/miniconda3/envs/*

Install package from conda-forge (larger, more up-to-date than default channel). Specific version: conda install package=1.2.3. Multiple: conda install pkg1 pkg2. -y skips confirmation. Use mamba instead of conda for much faster dependency solving.
Delete a conda environment completely. Cannot remove the currently active env (deactivate first). -y skips confirmation. Removes all installed packages in that env. Cannot be undone.

Export environment to YAML for reproducibility. Recreate: conda env create -f environment.yml. --no-builds for cross-platform compatibility. --from-history exports only explicitly installed packages (not auto-installed deps).
Print to stdout

make a new git repo from local. git init, git add . , git commit -m "comment", gh auto login, gh repo create YOUR_REPO_NAME --private --source=. --remote=origin --push. Don't forget to make gitignore file before git add
remove from git. remove the folder and everything inside it from Git only, and keeps it on the local device.

Mac stays awake for the duration of that utility. caffeinate -d : prevents the display from sleeping, caffeinate -i, prevents the system from idle sleep, caffeinate -m: prevents disk sleep. caffeinate -s: prevents system sleep on AC power. -t: you can set time(in second)

entries.command: ffprobe -v error -select_streams v:0 -show_entries stream=r_frame_rate -of default=nokey=1:noprint_wrappers=1 video.mp4
entries.description: Check fps of video
